mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed +0.5% in >60% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields positive forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side shocks that trigger short-term market panic and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day returns in >65% of observations during post-shock periods or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, monetary tightening pressures corporate margins and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of months where the rate exceeds the threshold or the signal's Sharpe ratio turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor strength signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of quarters or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth signals strengthening aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns or the information coefficient remains below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth during high inflation periods signals supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed +0.3% in >60% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds the threshold.
